title: glandularia andalgalensis
intro: Glandularia andalgalensis, also known as Andalgalopsis or Rockrose, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its pink or white flowers and fragrant, hairy leaves. The medicinal properties of Glandularia andalgalensis include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote respiratory health. Glandularia andalgalensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
